title: quercus pseudomolucca var. crassinervia
intro: Quercus pseudomolucca var. crassinervia, also known as the Philippine oak, is a variety of evergreen tree that belongs to the Quercus genus. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Quercus pseudomolucca var. crassinervia include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
